_note: AUTHORED flow (reviewed). The engine interprets it; it contains NO version data — it REFERENCES generated/magento-oss.json via registry.* refs. Prerequisites belong to the chosen version, shared across methods; credentials are per-step and just-in-time. The `location` step (name + empty-target) gates every acquisition — composer/git fail on a non-empty target, so we check first.

actions.label: Create a new Magento Open Source project

steps.label: How
steps.from: static:[clone-github:Clone from GitHub, composer:Create via Composer]

steps.label: Version
steps._note: clone allows a custom branch/ref; composer expects a release line

steps.label: Project location & name
steps._note: Collects ${parent} (folder to create in) + ${name}. empty-target FAILS if ${parent}/${name} already exists and is non-empty — composer create-project / git clone both refuse a non-empty target.

steps.label: Admin URL + credentials printed by setup:install

actions.label: Clone from a git URL (GitHub / Bitbucket / GitLab / self-hosted)
actions._note: User pastes their OWN repo URL (any host). The version is NOT known up front — it's read from THEIR composer.json after cloning, then mapped to requirements via the registry. Credential is HOST-SCOPED, resolved from the URL: ssh URL -> sshKey; private https -> gitHostAuth token; public -> none.

steps.label: Project location & name
steps._note: ${parent}/${name} must not exist or must be empty — git clone refuses a non-empty target.

steps.label: Auth for ${host.name} (SSH key or token) — secure form, host-scoped
Detect edition + version from THEIR composer.json
Resolve requirements for the detected version
Cloned + installed. Run bin/magento setup:install if this is a fresh DB.
